title: Cross-repo read-only collector with sibling resolution and dedup
description: Add an additive readonly open flag to FactStore, a sibling-repo resolver that turns hub.repos config (or discovered kb-* siblings) into absolute paths, and a collector that opens each sibling's derived FactStore read-only and pools their findings into one deduplicated Finding[] following the fleet synthesis.collect pattern. Extend `bober hub list` to aggregate across all resolved siblings.

successCriteria.description: FactStore accepts an optional { readonly?: boolean }; when true it opens better-sqlite3 with the readonly flag and skips the CREATE TABLE bootstrap, and when absent the constructor behaviour is unchanged from the prior version.
The collector pools findings from two distinct sibling FactStores into a single Finding[] whose length equals the number of distinct finding ids across both stores.
When the same Finding.id appears in two sibling stores the collector returns exactly one pooled entry for that id (dedup by id).
A collect over a sibling store opened through the collector leaves that sibling's facts.db file byte-unchanged (size and mtime stable) and a write attempt through the readonly handle is rejected.

The sibling resolver returns absolute paths from hub.repos config entries and, when that config is absent, discovers directories named kb-* beside the project root; a configured path that does not exist is skipped rather than throwing.
npm run build completes with zero TypeScript errors after the collector, resolver, and FactStore readonly flag are added.

nonGoals: Do not change FactStore behaviour for callers that omit the readonly flag; the default path stays byte-identical.
Do not implement scope parsing, the lens judge, or ranking (Sprint 3).
Do not write priority.md or add the priority/decide commands (Sprint 4).
Do not open sibling stores writable or run any migration against them.
Do not add a vault-markdown source; aggregation reads derived FactStores only.
stopConditions: The readonly FactStore flag, the sibling resolver, and the collector exist; `bober hub list` aggregates across resolved siblings; and all six success criteria pass evaluation.
npm run build exits zero and the existing FactStore tests plus the new collector tests pass.
definitionOfDone: The hub can resolve sibling kb-* repos to absolute paths, open each one's derived FactStore read-only, and pool their findings into one Finding[] deduplicated by id, without ever mutating a sibling file. `bober hub list` now reflects findings aggregated across every resolved sibling. The additive FactStore readonly flag leaves existing callers byte-identical, the build is green, and the new tests pass.
assumptions: Sibling repos are sibling clones next to the project root, resolved to absolute paths (research:109-112; resolveBlackboardPath ABSOLUTE discipline from fleet memory).
The collector mirrors fleet synthesis.collect (src/fleet/synthesis.ts:29-39): pure data shaping, no LLM, no network.
better-sqlite3 supports a readonly open flag, so the additive FactStore change is small and safe (src/state/facts.ts:139-167).
Each sibling's derived FactStore exposes findings at predicate 'finding' (Sprint 1 source contract).
outOfScope: Reading the canonical vault markdown (owned by obsidian-vault-store).
generatorNotes: FactStore change (src/state/facts.ts): add readonly?: boolean to the opts type at line 140; when true call new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true }) and skip the this.db.exec(CREATE TABLE...) block (lines 150-166); leave the WAL/busy_timeout branches and the no-flag path untouched. Add src/hub/repo-resolver.ts: read config.hub?.repos (string[]) if present, else readdir the parent of projectRoot and keep entries matching /^kb-/ that are directories; resolve all to absolute paths via path.resolve; filter to existing facts.db locations; skip non-existent. Reuse factsDbPath (src/state/facts.ts:77) to locate each sibling's derived store. Add src/hub/collector.ts exporting collectFindings(repoPaths, scope) that opens each store with new FactStore(dbPath, { readonly: true }), wraps it in the Sprint 1 FactStoreFindingSource, concatenates all Findings, then dedups by Finding.id (keep first; stable order). Model the pooling shape on src/fleet/synthesis.ts:29-39 and the readAll convention in src/fleet/shared-blackboard.ts:103-105. Extend src/cli/commands/hub.ts `list` to resolve siblings and collect across them. Guard every store open in try/catch so a missing or corrupt sibling is skipped, not fatal.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm the no-flag FactStore constructor still creates the table (existing tests green) and that { readonly: true } both skips CREATE TABLE and opens readonly. Seed two temp FactStores with overlapping and distinct finding ids; assert pooled length equals distinct-id count and the overlapping id appears once. Snapshot a sibling facts.db size+mtime before and after a collect and assert unchanged; assert a write through the readonly handle throws. Point the resolver at a temp parent dir containing kb-a and kb-b plus a non-kb dir and a non-existent configured path; assert only the existing kb-* absolute paths are returned. Confirm npm run build is green.
